title: Define structured PR task metadata
goal: Define and implement a structured Bureau task marker contract for PRs so duplicate detection prefers explicit metadata over broad title/body heuristics.

metadata.source_review: Follow-up from PR #177 open-PR claim guard review.
metadata.safety_boundary: Metadata improves duplicate detection but does not grant PR creation, review or merge authority.
metadata.outcome: Implemented in Bureau PR #224: open PR task binding now prefers structured PR body lines, labels and structured API metadata over broad title/body/branch heuristics; the contract is documented and covered by claim-guard and GitHub observer tests.
validation: Bureau PR #224 GitHub CI passed validate (3.10) and validate (3.12).
Local before merge: PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m bureau.cli --root . check -> valid True.
Local before merge: PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m pytest -> 354 passed.
Local before merge: PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m ruff check . -> passed.
Local before merge: PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m bureau.cli --root . --json github-observe --repo heimgewebe/bureau -> healthy true, binding_healthy true.
Local before merge: PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m bureau.cli --root . --json doctor -> healthy true.
Local before merge: git diff --check -> passed.
Post-merge: python3 -m bureau.cli --root . check -> valid True.
Post-merge: doctor healthy true and github-observe binding_healthy true.

verified_acceptance.metadata-contract: docs/contracts/open-pr-task-metadata-v1.md documents structured body, label and metadata markers, including multi-task forms.
verified_acceptance.heuristic-order: src/bureau/v2.py uses structured PR task metadata before title/body/branch heuristics; src/bureau/github_observer.py requests labels and binds structured markers before branch fallback.
verified_acceptance.grammar-tests: Existing boundary tests for T001, T0010, T001-EXTRA and branch suffix behavior remain; new tests cover structured label override and multi-task metadata.

acceptance.assertion: A documented PR marker such as a label or body field binds a PR to exactly one or more Bureau task IDs.
The Open-PR guard prefers structured markers and treats title/body/branch scanning as explicit fallback with lower confidence diagnostics.
Tests cover T001, T0010, T001-EXTRA and branch suffix cases according to the documented task-ID grammar.
